differences.title: 1. COMPILATION
differences.leftDesc: Svelte compiles code to imperative vanilla JS at build time, no runtime needed.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js uses a Virtual DOM for efficient UI updates, which has a runtime cost.

differences.title: 2. REACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Reactivity is baked into Svelte's compiler, making it very efficient and fast.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js uses Proxies for reactivity, offering a familiar and powerful system.

differences.title: 3. BUNDLE SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's compiler approach results in significantly smaller JavaScript bundles for applications.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js bundles are generally larger due to its runtime and Virtual DOM.

differences.title: 4. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's syntax is close to plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, easy to pick up.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js has an approachable API, especially for those familiar with component-based UIs.

differences.title: 5. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's lack of a virtual DOM and runtime leads to superior performance benchmarks.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js offers excellent performance, with optimizations for complex applications.

differences.title: 6. ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's ecosystem is expanding rapidly with community-driven tools and libraries.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js boasts a large, mature ecosystem with extensive official and community support.

differences.title: 7. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Svelte offers simple, built-in stores for efficient state management.
differences.rightDesc: Vue.js relies on dedicated libraries like Pinia or Vuex for robust state management.
